speaker: Ruth Chang
episode_or_content_title: How to make hard choices (TED Talk, 2014)
verification_notes: Created 2026-05-26 via the Six-State Buildout (Phase 8 human-gated promotion). prototype_only reconstruction — paraphrase of the speaker's documented idea; NOT a verified verbatim excerpt. Ruth Chang is a philosopher at Cornell Law School (formerly Rutgers), known for her work on incommensurability and practical reason. Her 2014 TEDSalon NY talk is the canonical public expression of her "hard choices" argument, cited extensively in philosophy and popular press. Tier-1 (academic philosopher + TED platform). The "we make ourselves" framing and "on a par" concept are documented in multiple secondary sources confirming Chang genuinely holds and expressed these ideas in this talk.
